name: Stale-base sibling merges silently revert state files unless rebased
description: When the coordinator dispatches parallel leads, each lead's worktree is created from main HEAD AT DISPATCH TIME. As sibling leads merge, the laggers' branches stay rooted at the old base. Their git diff vs main shows phantom 'reversions' of state files (.seeds/issues.jsonl, .overstory/groups.json, .mulch/expertise/*.jsonl) and even of newly-merged source files. ov merge's 3-way merge usually handles source files correctly (additions on main side win when branch has no opinion), but state files where both sides edited the same lines can silently produce bad merges. Always check git diff main..branch BEFORE merging a lead's branch — if it shows ANY state-file delta or any deletion of a recently-merged file, defer and rebase first. The clean recovery pattern: spawn a single builder with no --base-branch, have it git cherry-pick the lead's commits onto current main, verify diff is source-only, then merge that branch instead.

name: Recovery after mass agent kill: builders' commits survive on their child branches
description: When all leads in a swarm die simultaneously (e.g. operator hits Claude Code rate limit), the leads' lead branches typically have no commits, but the BUILDERS' child branches preserve the actual implementation work. Recovery pattern: stop zombie leads with ov stop --clean-worktree, spawn fresh recovery leads with --base-branch <builder-branch> on the same task IDs, dispatch them with VERIFY-AND-MERGE instructions (not redo). This salvages all completed code and only re-runs quality gates. Verified in phase3 swarm: Lead A's conn-builder commit a52f5ea and Lead B's dashboard-headless-builder commit 456a9fe both survived rate-limit kill and were merged via recovery leads after task reopen.

name: Coordinator direct-builder spawn for narrow rebase or doc work
description: Standard rule is 'coordinator spawns leads only.' For very narrow work (cherry-picking a single commit onto current main, writing a single doc file), direct ov sling --capability builder --depth 1 is more efficient: avoids the lead+builder overhead and the lead overlay's 'sd close' step that fights dispatch. Used 4 times in phase3 swarm: hooks-design-doc-rebaser (cherry-pick design doc), nudge-rebaser, watchdog-rebaser (both cherry-pick lead work onto current main), hooks-design-doc-writer (originally tried as direct builder for doc writing). The direct-builder dispatch should be terse: 'cherry-pick X onto main, verify diff is exactly Y files, send merge_ready, do NOT close issue.' Coordinator constraints prohibit the coordinator itself from cherry-picking, so a 1-builder spawn is the right tool.
